title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Browser and Node Client Syste… · Compiler Case: Streaming Uploads
summary: A media uploader streams files and reads large responses through Web Streams without accidental cloning, locked bodies, or unbounded decoding. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts streaming uploads increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded streaming body pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Predict Streaming Uploads · Theory Streaming Uploads
steps.instruction:
theory case — TypeScript Fetch evidence model streams a 12 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 30 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup. Competency probe: Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
